purpose: Operator-pickable target feature for TASK-HMIG-010's end-to-end LangGraph validation run. Mirrors the canary-set.json pattern from TASK-HMIG-009: candidates with rationale, fit scoring against AC-001, and an operator_pick slot that the operator fills in BEFORE running guardkit autobuild feature.

update_history.change: Dropped beads-integration candidate (operator: never going to need it). Dropped feature-build-ux candidate (Claude-Code-tied surfaces — TTY/progress/polling — that LangGraph Player can't meaningfully exercise). Promoted autobuild-observability-fixes to rank 1 (cleanest fit for runtime-agnostic orchestrator validation). Added graphiti-docs at rank 2 (safer/lighter alternative). Demoted graphiti-context-fixes to rank 3 (too small). Added 'design-fresh-via-feature-spec' as an alternative path.

ac_001_selection_criteria.note_on_criteria_strictness: These criteria together are tight. As of 2026-06-04 no surviving backlog feature satisfies all five — the BDD-gated requirement is the constraint that no planned feature meets after the runtime-agnostic filter (the only BDD-gated backlog feature was beads-integration, which the operator has explicitly dropped). Operator MUST either (a) relax the BDD criterion with rationale (the 009A 12-run batch already validated the BDD-plugin contract C1 under qwen36-workhorse — re-exercising at feature scale adds little marginal coverage beyond the WorktreeManager + feature_complete paths), or (b) take the 'design-fresh-via-feature-spec' alternative path which naturally generates Gherkin scenarios. Record the chosen deviation rationale in operator_pick.criteria_deviation.

fit_against_ac_001.state_bridge_tasks: Yes — TASK-FIX-GD02 (shared-worktree git detection baseline) touches the WorktreeManager + state_bridge boundary directly
fit_against_ac_001.verdict: Fits structure + effort + state-bridge; FAILS BDD criterion (relaxable, see ac_001_selection_criteria.note_on_criteria_strictness)

task_breakdown.title: Exclude internal artifacts from documentation constraint count
Scope git detection to per-task file changes in shared worktrees
Add independent test execution for testing task type

candidates.rationale: RECOMMENDED. The cleanest fit on every criterion except BDD: 3 tasks, 2 waves, ~6-8h, state-bridge boundary directly exercised (GD02), all three tasks are pure orchestrator/server-side logic agnostic to whether the Player runs under Claude Code or LangGraph. Real delivery value: improves the autobuild orchestrator surface that TASK-HMIG-010 is itself validating — dogfooding synergy. Per-task tasks_to_test_paths are all visible in the Player's worktree (no external dependencies on Claude Code, IDE, TTY, etc).
candidates.why_runtime_agnostic: GD02 = git detection logic; IA03 = doc-constraint counting; TP05 = test execution for testing task type. None depend on the runtime context (TTY/IDE/progress display) the way the previously-considered feature-build-ux did. The LangGraph Player can write + test these changes inside its worktree exactly the same way a Claude Code Player can.

fit_against_ac_001.verdict: Fits structure + effort; FAILS BDD + state-bridge

candidates.rationale: SAFER/LIGHTER alternative. Pure documentation work — easiest for the Player to complete successfully (low risk of F6 honesty collapse since there's no functional code to lie about). Wave 2 (DOC-004 nav) depends on Wave 1's three parallel doc-writes, exercising the WorktreeManager merge path. Caveat: documentation-only doesn't stress the Coach's test-execution gates the way GD02 would, so it under-validates a chunk of what TASK-HMIG-010 is supposed to measure. Pick this if minimising run-time risk matters more than orchestration-surface coverage.
candidates.why_runtime_agnostic: Markdown file writes. No runtime context dependencies.

fit_against_ac_001.state_bridge_tasks: No — these are pure CLI + Graphiti-integration tasks
fit_against_ac_001.estimated_orchestrator_hours: 2-4 (complexity 1+3+7, but the 7-complexity one is review_complete so effectively 1+3=4)
fit_against_ac_001.verdict: Fits structure; UNDERSHOOTS effort (too small to exercise feature-build at load); FAILS BDD + state-bridge

candidates.rationale: Listed for completeness as the smallest 3-task / 2-wave backlog candidate. Probably too lightweight to meaningfully validate the WorktreeManager + feature_complete + Coach-gating-across-multiple-tasks paths the task is designed to exercise. Not recommended unless operator wants a fast, low-cost smoke and is willing to accept that 010 will not stress-test the orchestration surface.

alternative_path_design_fresh.summary: Spec + plan a fresh small feature using /feature-spec + /feature-plan, then run autobuild against the freshly-designed feature. Adds ~1-2h of design overhead before the autobuild run starts but naturally produces BDD-gated tasks (via /feature-spec's Gherkin generation), satisfying AC-001 cleanly without relaxation.
alternative_path_design_fresh.when_to_choose: If you want the AC-001 BDD criterion satisfied without operator hand-waving, AND you have a small (≤8h) feature idea you'd actually want delivered.
alternative_path_design_fresh.cost: ~1-2h /feature-spec + /feature-plan time before autobuild starts. Total wall-clock budget remains comfortably within the 2026-06-15 deadline.
alternative_path_design_fresh.what_it_doesnt_solve: The runtime-agnostic concern still applies — the feature idea must not be Claude-Code-tied (TTY, progress, IDE integration). Pick a feature that lives in pure orchestrator/CLI/library code.
candidate_feature_ideas: Operator does not currently have a small ≤8h fresh feature in mind. /task-work scaffold cannot recommend one without business context. If choosing this path, the operator should propose a feature idea, and /feature-spec + /feature-plan will land 3-5 BDD-gated tasks shaped to it.
alternative_path_design_fresh.execution_sketch: 1. Operator: 'I want to spec X.' 2. /feature-spec X --auto produces .feature file in docs/bdd/. 3. /feature-plan X --context docs/bdd/X.feature produces tasks/backlog/X/{README,IMPLEMENTATION-GUIDE,TASK-*}.md. 4. Update this feature-target.json:operator_pick to point at the newly-created feature. 5. Run guardkit autobuild feature X under LangGraph per the execution_plan_once_picked steps.

operator_pick.slice_note: Full feature — no slicing. All 3 tasks across 2 waves (Wave 1: IA03; Wave 2: GD02, TP05).

criteria_deviation.rationale: No backlog feature outside the dropped beads-integration has a BDD-gated task. Relaxation is acceptable because TASK-HMIG-009A's 2026-06-04 12-run batch already validated the BDD-plugin contract C1 under qwen36-workhorse end-to-end. Re-exercising the BDD-plugin path at feature scale adds little marginal coverage beyond the WorktreeManager + feature_complete + multi-task Coach-gating paths, which the picked feature exercises through GD02 (state-bridge) + TP05 (test execution) + multi-task wave structure.

operator_pick.rationale: Recommended pick. Real autobuild-surface delivery value (dogfooding: the feature improves the autobuild orchestrator that TASK-HMIG-010 itself validates). Runtime-agnostic — all three tasks are pure orchestrator/server-side logic with no Claude-Code/TTY/IDE dependencies. State-bridge boundary directly exercised by GD02 (shared-worktree git detection). Fits ≤8h orchestrator-time bound. 3 tasks across 2 waves provides multi-wave Coach-gating coverage.

execution_plan_once_picked.step_1_baseline: Confirm the picked tasks are clean in backlog (not in_progress, not blocked). If a slice is being used, file a placeholder note in the feature folder's README documenting which tasks are in/out of the 010 slice so subsequent /feature-build runs don't re-pick them.
execution_plan_once_picked.step_3_run: GUARDKIT_HARNESS=langgraph OPENAI_BASE_URL=http://promaxgb10-41b1:9000/v1 OPENAI_API_KEY=llama-swap-local-key guardkit autobuild feature {feature_id} --model qwen36-workhorse 2>&1 | tee .guardkit/autobuild/TASK-REV-HMIG-feature-run/run-1-stdout.log
execution_plan_once_picked.step_4_per_task_record: After each task completes (success or failure), append a record to TASK-REV-HMIG-feature-results.json matching the AC-003 schema. The runner does not yet auto-emit this — manual append is the current shape.
execution_plan_once_picked.step_5_resume_on_failure: Any first-pass failure → guardkit autobuild feature {feature_id} --resume. Record resume outcome and first-pass-fail reason in the same per-task record.
execution_plan_once_picked.step_6_merge: On all-pass: guardkit autobuild complete {feature_id}. Confirm no merge conflicts, no broken downstream consumers (AC-007).
execution_plan_once_picked.step_7_analysis: Append narrative to docs/state/TASK-REV-HMIG/feature-run-analysis.md per AC-009. Compare first-pass-success rate to 009A's 67% LangGraph baseline (AC-004). Compute falsifier verdict per AC-008.

model_choice.rationale: Same model as 009A's validated batch. No re-validation needed; the 009A 12-run batch proved this substrate satisfies the Player+Coach contract end-to-end under both wire formats.

notes_for_operator: AC-001 selection is the load-bearing decision in this task. The /task-work scaffold has presented 3 backlog candidates + 1 alternative path; pick one and fill the operator_pick block.

RECOMMENDED: autobuild-observability-fixes (rank 1). Real autobuild-surface delivery value, runtime-agnostic, exercises state-bridge (GD02), fits ≤8h. Requires relaxing the BDD criterion — rationale is the 009A 12-run batch already validated the BDD-plugin contract under qwen36-workhorse.
Dropped from earlier scaffold: beads-integration (operator: never going to need); feature-build-ux (Claude-Code-tied surfaces that the LangGraph Player can't meaningfully exercise).
Falsifier verdict (AC-008): ≥80% first-pass-success AND no non-recoverable failure → proceed to Wave 4 cutover (TASK-HMIG-011). Anything else → halt cutover, escalate.
Schedule: spec says D-9 → D-7 so results inform the D-7 cutover-flip decision. Deadline is 2026-06-15 so D-7 ≈ 2026-06-08; budget ~2-3 days for the run + analysis.
